core_payload.content:
# Tracker Historical Intelligence

## 1. Role
Tracker Historical Intelligence owns the long-memory layer of execution evidence. It keeps metrics, analytics, and archive memory together so follow-through can be studied over time rather than judged only in the present tense.

## 2. Historical Intelligence Surface
This sub-hub groups `metric`, `analytics`, and `archive`. Together they define how Tracker preserves time-series evidence, supports retrospection, and keeps completion history available for planning improvement, governance review, and pattern learning.

## 3. Operating Boundary
This sub-hub does not own live reminder timing, object-state tracking, or direct runtime intervention. Those responsibilities belong to `schedule-pressure`, `tracked-objects`, and `orchestration-signals`.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Tracker Historical Intelligence groups the long-memory surfaces of the tracker system: Metric, Analytics, and Archive. It keeps time-series evidence, retrospection, and preserved execution history together so follow-through can be studied across longer arcs rather than only in the present tense. This boundary matters because better planning and better intervention depend on historical evidence staying coherent, queryable, and distinct from live timing pressure. It gives the tracker stack one explicit memory layer for pattern learning, retrospection, and durable execution context.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted during Tracker decomposition to isolate metrics, analytics, and archive memory from live pressure and orchestration signal surfaces.
